insulin_sufficiency.summary: Overall insulin delivery shows opportunity for improvement (TIR 75%, TBR<70 3.2%, TAR>180 22%). 1 risk(s) identified; 0 parameter change(s) proposed this cycle.

basal.summary: Basal: no change recommended (insufficient evidence (confidence 0.22 below 0.50)).
basal.evidence: Consolidated from 3 time-block advisories (range: 2-40%). Overnight quadrant analysis (EXP-2589): BASAL TOO HIGH. [CLAMPED from 34% to 25% — stage over multiple adjustment cycles] [Controller: Loop]
basal.justification: Basal reviewed: a directional signal exists (theoretical target 0.36) but is held due to insufficient evidence (confidence 0.22 below 0.50). Re-evaluate at the next review.

success: Basal held: glycemic metrics remain within tolerance of baseline over the 2-week window.
No new hypoglycemia signal (TBR<70 stays below 4%).
stop_escalate: TBR<70 rises by more than 1 pp -> escalate review.
TIR declines materially -> re-open Basal for change.

isf.summary: ISF: no change recommended (insufficient evidence (confidence 0.13 below 0.50)).
isf.evidence: Consolidated from 6 time-block advisories (range: 50-187%). Circadian ISF analysis (EXP-2271): day insulin is 1. [CLAMPED from 72% to 25% — stage over multiple adjustment cycles] [Controller: Loop]
isf.justification: ISF reviewed: a directional signal exists (theoretical target 98) but is held due to insufficient evidence (confidence 0.13 below 0.50). Re-evaluate at the next review.

success: ISF held: glycemic metrics remain within tolerance of baseline over the 2-week window.
No new hypoglycemia signal (TBR<70 stays below 4%).
stop_escalate: TBR<70 rises by more than 1 pp -> escalate review.
TIR declines materially -> re-open ISF for change.

cr.summary: Carb ratio: no change recommended (insufficient evidence (confidence 0.26 below 0.50)).
cr.evidence: Consolidated from 3 time-block advisories (range: 9-24%). Context-aware CR analysis (EXP-2341): morning meals show mean excursion 83 mg/dL from 264 meals. [Controller: Loop]
cr.justification: Carb ratio reviewed: a directional signal exists (theoretical target 6.7) but is held due to insufficient evidence (confidence 0.26 below 0.50). Re-evaluate at the next review.

success: Carb ratio held: glycemic metrics remain within tolerance of baseline over the 2-week window.
No new hypoglycemia signal (TBR<70 stays below 4%).
stop_escalate: TBR<70 rises by more than 1 pp -> escalate review.
TIR declines materially -> re-open Carb ratio for change.

overall_justification: No parameter changes are recommended this cycle; all domains were reviewed and held with documented rationale. Held/deferred: Basal (insufficient_evidence), ISF (insufficient_evidence), Carb ratio (insufficient_evidence).
addenda: Factors considered: time-in-range distribution, hypo/hyper burden, glycemic variability, per-parameter advisory evidence, and cross-parameter sequencing.
Basal theoretical optimum: 0.36 (-40% vs current 0.6); held this cycle.
ISF theoretical optimum: 98 (+51% vs current 65); held this cycle.
Carb ratio theoretical optimum: 6.7 (-21% vs current 8.5); held this cycle.
Risks reviewed and mitigated: Glycemic variability high (CV 41% vs target 36%).

Mitigations: changes are bounded by a per-cycle titration cap; carb ratio is sequenced after basal/ISF to avoid confounded adjustment; explicit stop/escalate criteria accompany every recommendation for the 2-week feedback loop.

reboot.rationale: Settings reboot not indicated: the severe-mismatch composite (burden + large mismatch + low consistency) is not met. Incremental titration is appropriate.

reimbursement.data_sufficiency: Analysis based on 180 days of CGM data (46,151 readings). Sufficient for time-in-range and titration assessment.
mitigations: Recommendations bounded by a safe per-cycle titration cap.
Carb ratio sequenced after basal/ISF to prevent confounded change.
Explicit stop/escalate criteria defined for each recommendation.
alternatives_discussed: Considered no-change vs incremental titration vs settings reboot.
Theoretical optima documented but deferred in favor of safe steps.
patient_barriers: No patient-reported adherence, supply, or prescription barriers noted at this review.
reimbursement.agreed_plan: Agreed plan: maintain current settings with documented rationale; re-evaluate in 2 weeks.
reimbursement.expected_trajectory: Projected time-in-range at next review: ~75% (baseline 75%). Outcome will be scored against the per-recommendation success and stop/escalate criteria.

figures.title: Time-in-range distribution
figures.caption: Share of time in each glycemic band over 46,151 readings. Green is the 70-180 mg/dL target; reds are lows, ambers are highs.
figures.alt: Stacked bar of time spent in each glucose range.

figures.title: Ambulatory glucose profile (AGP)
figures.caption: Median glucose by time of day with interquartile (25–75%) and 10–90% bands. The green zone is the 70–180 mg/dL target; a flat median inside it is the goal.
figures.alt: Ambulatory glucose profile percentile bands by hour of day.

figures.title: Overnight glucose profile (00:00–06:00)
figures.caption: Median overnight glucose with IQR. A rising trend suggests basal is too low; a falling trend suggests it is too high. Used to contextualize the basal recommendation.
figures.alt: Overnight median glucose by hour with interquartile band.

figures.title: Post-meal glucose excursion
figures.caption: Median glucose rise after 255 carb-counted, bolused meals (peak +38 mg/dL at 60 min; +20 mg/dL vs baseline at 4 h). Carb ratio held this cycle; this profile is the baseline to compare against at the next review.
figures.alt: Mean post-meal glucose excursion curve with interquartile band.

figures.title: Demand-phase ISF decomposition
figures.caption: Profile ISF vs the apparent/correction ISF (amplified by AID compensation) vs the demand-phase ISF — the validated 0–2h insulin effect (EXP-2651) and the true target, shown with its 95% confidence interval. The apparent value is not the target; the recommendation tracks the demand-phase value, bounded by a safety margin (EXP-2738).
figures.alt: Bar chart of profile, apparent, and demand-phase ISF with CI.

figures.title: Scheduled vs actual basal
figures.caption: Median programmed basal vs what the loop actually delivered by hour. Persistent loop deviation indicates the scheduled rate is mismatched in that direction.

figures.title: ISF reconciliation (profile vs observed)
figures.caption: Profile ISF vs the correction-derived (observed/apparent) ISF. The observed value is amplified by AID compensation (basal suspension during corrections), so it is NOT a direct ISF target: the recommendation deliberately preserves the controller's residual safety margin (EXP-2738) rather than chasing the apparent value. Separately, a lower effective ISF for large single corrections is dose-shaping guidance (split the dose), not a baseline schedule change.
